articles.title: GPU at $2.25/HR or $12.29/HR: The Infrastructure Layer That Determines Price
articles.summary: The 9x price spread on H100 is real but the comparison requires some care. The $1.38/hr end is typically reserved or committed capacity. The $12.29/hr end is on demand at major cloud providers with full flexibility premium built in.The more meaningful comparison is 3-year TCO for a team running consistent utilization. At 85% utilization on 1,000 GPUs, dedicated colocated infrastructure in a secondary market typically runs 40-60% of equivalent cloud cost after accounting for all non-compute costs

articles.title: Show HN: HIC – Same-Ring Isolation, 4ns IPC, Runs on 8086
articles.summary: HIC is a capability-secure OS kernel. It isolates system services inside Ring 0. Each service occupies a separate physical address range. The MMU enforces the boundary.There is no kernel IPC path. A cross-domain call is three instructions: call into an entry page, bt to test a domain ID against an authorization bitmap, jmp into the business logic. No privilege switch occurs. No kernel code runs during communication. Parameters travel in registers or shared memory.A caller cannot name the address
